title: COMPAT — Constraint-Market Compatibility: Industrial Process Variable Instruments (NAICS 334513)

sections.title: Step 07 — Product Constraints (from upstream)
sections.content:
Constraints carried forward from 03_constraints.md. No web search augmentation per instructions.

| # | Constraint | Type | is_absolute | Threshold | Unit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Aqueous Media Requirement | physical | **true** | — | — |
| C2 | Maximum Medium Temperature | physical | false | 90 | °C |
| C3 | Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit | physical | **true** | 2 | % vol |
| C4 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | physical | false | 1.5 (uncompensated) | % error |
| C5 | Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility | chemical | false | — | — |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition Required | operational | **true** | 100 | % fill |
| C7 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | operational | false | 0.25–4.2 | l/min |
| C8 | Maximum Operating Pressure | operational | false | 10 | bar |
| C9 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | operational | false | 5–10 | × DN |
| C10 | Life Endurance Ceiling | economic | false | 3,000 | m³ total |
| C11 | No Explosive Atmosphere Certification | regulatory | false | Zone 1/0 | ATEX zone |
| C12 | Ambient Temperature Range | environmental | false | −20 to +60 (est.) | °C ambient |

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Constraint-Market Assessment: NAICS 334513
sections.content:
**Market context:** NAICS 334513 is Marquardt's home industry — the manufacturing of instruments that measure, display, and control industrial process variables. This VN encompasses all flow instrument technologies (ultrasonic, electromagnetic, Coriolis, vortex, turbine, differential-pressure, thermal mass), plus pressure, temperature, level, analytical, and control instruments. Marquardt occupies the ultrasonic transit-time inline flowmeter niche within this NAICS, targeting OEM integration at DN12–DN25 with polymer housing and $20–60 price points. [SRC: VN_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_]

Operating environment: Indoor factory/production environments. Calibration rigs use water at 5–50 °C and 0–10 bar. Test benches circulate water or water-glycol through DN15–DN100 closed-loop piping at controlled flow rates. Assembly and integration stations operate at ambient indoor conditions (18–28 °C, 30–70% RH). [SRC: VN L6.5, L6.6]

### Verdict Matrix

| # | Constraint | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Aqueous Media Requirement | **none** | Marquardt's product segment within 334513 uses water and water-glycol as calibration and test media. Wet calibration rigs (L5.6.1) circulate clean water through closed-loop test circuits. The home-market product targets water-based applications by design. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01] |
| C2 | Max Medium Temperature (90 °C) | **none** | Calibration and test circuits operate at 5–50 °C. Environmental testing (L5.6.2) cycles to 90 °C maximum — matching the sensor's rated limit exactly. No routine process within this market's instrument manufacturing chain exceeds 90 °C for the sensor's wetted path. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01 specs] |
| C3 | Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit | **none** | Calibration rigs (L5.6.1) use de-aerated, temperature-stabilized water to ensure reference-grade measurement accuracy. Air content is actively minimized to <0.1% to avoid calibration error. Production test loops are closed-loop, pressurized systems. [SRC: VN L5.6.1] |
| C4 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | **none** | Calibration is performed at controlled, stable temperatures with known fluid properties. NTC compensation reduces temperature-induced error to ~0.2%. No extreme thermal transients in instrument manufacturing test environments. [SRC: 01 constraints, VN L6.6] |
| C5 | Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility | **none** | Calibration and test media are clean water or water-glycol — no aggressive chemicals contact the sensor during manufacturing or end-use calibration. Polymer housing and KTW/WRAS-approved seals are fully compatible. [SRC: 01 features, VN L5.6.1] |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition Required | **none** | All calibration rigs and test loops are closed-pipe, fully filled, and pressurized systems. Fill/drain procedures ensure complete pipe-full condition before any measurement. Partially filled pipes would invalidate calibration references. [SRC: VN L5.6.1] |
| C7 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | **none** | Calibration rigs cover the sensor's specified range (0.25–60 l/min depending on DN variant) with variable-speed pumps. The calibration process explicitly characterizes the sensor across its operating range including low-flow points. [SRC: VN L4.6.1.2, 01 specs] |
| C8 | Max Operating Pressure (10 bar) | **none** | Calibration loops typically operate at 1–6 bar. Pressure cycle endurance testing (L5.6.2) goes to rated pressure (10 bar) and burst test at 1.5× — the test infrastructure is designed to match the product's rated envelope. [SRC: VN L4.6.2.2, 01 specs] |
| C9 | Min Straight Pipe Run (5–10× DN) | **none** | Calibration rigs (L5.6.1) are purpose-built with adequate straight-run sections upstream and downstream of the device-under-test position. Straight pipe run is a standard rig design consideration, not a constraint in this market. [SRC: VN L4.6.1.2] |
| C10 | Life Endurance Ceiling (3,000 m³) | **none** | In this market, the sensor is the manufactured product, not a consumed component. Life endurance is a specification the manufacturer validates (L5.6.2), not a constraint that limits market participation. Each individual sensor goes through a calibration run consuming a few liters, not thousands of cubic meters. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01 specs] |
| C11 | No ATEX Certification | **none** | Instrument manufacturing facilities (L6.1–L6.7) are not classified as explosive atmospheres. No Zone 0/1/2 classification applies to standard instrument production floors, calibration labs, or assembly areas. [SRC: VN L6.10] |
| C12 | Ambient Temperature Range | **none** | All manufacturing, calibration, and testing occurs in indoor, climate-controlled environments (18–28 °C ambient). RAST 2.5 connector and polymer housing are designed for exactly this type of protected installation environment. [SRC: VN L6.5, 01 specs] |

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total assessments** | 12 |
| **Knockouts** | 0 |
| **Mitigable** | 0 |
| **None (irrelevant)** | 12 |
| **Market status** | **SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring |

**Conclusion:** NAICS 334513 is Marquardt's home industry with architecture distance = 0. All 12 constraints are irrelevant — the product was designed for and within this market. Calibration rigs use water at controlled conditions, manufacturing occurs indoors, no explosive atmospheres apply, and life endurance is a specification to validate rather than a consumption limit. Zero constraints require mitigation. This is the baseline against which all other markets are compared. [SRC: VN_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_, 03_constraints, 01_product_decomposition]

---

rows.Rationale: Marquardt's product segment within 334513 uses water and water-glycol as calibration and test media. Wet calibration rigs (L5.6.1) circulate clean water through closed-loop test circuits. The home-market product targets water-based applications by design. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01]

Calibration and test circuits operate at 5–50 °C. Environmental testing (L5.6.2) cycles to 90 °C maximum — matching the sensor's rated limit exactly. No routine process within this market's instrument manufacturing chain exceeds 90 °C for the sensor's wetted path. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01 specs]

Calibration rigs (L5.6.1) use de-aerated, temperature-stabilized water to ensure reference-grade measurement accuracy. Air content is actively minimized to <0.1% to avoid calibration error. Production test loops are closed-loop, pressurized systems. [SRC: VN L5.6.1]

Calibration is performed at controlled, stable temperatures with known fluid properties. NTC compensation reduces temperature-induced error to ~0.2%. No extreme thermal transients in instrument manufacturing test environments. [SRC: 01 constraints, VN L6.6]

Calibration and test media are clean water or water-glycol — no aggressive chemicals contact the sensor during manufacturing or end-use calibration. Polymer housing and KTW/WRAS-approved seals are fully compatible. [SRC: 01 features, VN L5.6.1]

All calibration rigs and test loops are closed-pipe, fully filled, and pressurized systems. Fill/drain procedures ensure complete pipe-full condition before any measurement. Partially filled pipes would invalidate calibration references. [SRC: VN L5.6.1]

Calibration rigs cover the sensor's specified range (0.25–60 l/min depending on DN variant) with variable-speed pumps. The calibration process explicitly characterizes the sensor across its operating range including low-flow points. [SRC: VN L4.6.1.2, 01 specs]

Calibration loops typically operate at 1–6 bar. Pressure cycle endurance testing (L5.6.2) goes to rated pressure (10 bar) and burst test at 1.5× — the test infrastructure is designed to match the product's rated envelope. [SRC: VN L4.6.2.2, 01 specs]

Calibration rigs (L5.6.1) are purpose-built with adequate straight-run sections upstream and downstream of the device-under-test position. Straight pipe run is a standard rig design consideration, not a constraint in this market. [SRC: VN L4.6.1.2]

In this market, the sensor is the manufactured product, not a consumed component. Life endurance is a specification the manufacturer validates (L5.6.2), not a constraint that limits market participation. Each individual sensor goes through a calibration run consuming a few liters, not thousands of cubic meters. [SRC: VN L6.6, 01 specs]

Instrument manufacturing facilities (L6.1–L6.7) are not classified as explosive atmospheres. No Zone 0/1/2 classification applies to standard instrument production floors, calibration labs, or assembly areas. [SRC: VN L6.10]

All manufacturing, calibration, and testing occurs in indoor, climate-controlled environments (18–28 °C ambient). RAST 2.5 connector and polymer housing are designed for exactly this type of protected installation environment. [SRC: VN L6.5, 01 specs]

rows.Value: **SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring

assessments.rationale: Home market uses water and water-glycol as calibration and test media. Product targets water-based applications by design.
Calibration circuits 5-50 degC. Environmental testing cycles to 90 degC max — matching rated limit. No routine process exceeds 90 degC.
Calibration rigs use de-aerated water at <0.1% air content. Closed-loop pressurized systems.
Calibration at controlled, stable temperatures. NTC compensation to ~0.2%. No extreme transients in manufacturing test environments.
Clean water or water-glycol test media. Polymer housing and KTW/WRAS seals fully compatible.
Calibration rigs and test loops are closed-pipe, fully filled, pressurized systems. Standard requirement for valid calibration.
Calibration rigs cover the full specified flow range with variable-speed pumps. Low-flow characterization is part of the process.
Calibration loops at 1-6 bar. Pressure endurance testing at rated 10 bar. Test infrastructure matches product envelope.
Calibration rigs are purpose-built with adequate straight-run sections. Standard rig design consideration, not a market constraint.
Sensor is the manufactured product, not a consumed component. Life endurance is validated as a specification, not consumed during production.
Instrument manufacturing facilities are not classified as explosive atmospheres. No ATEX Zone 0/1/2 applies to production floors or calibration labs.
Indoor, climate-controlled manufacturing and testing at 18-28 degC ambient. RAST 2.5 connector designed for this environment.
